joke: Why don’t scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
Alright, fair enough. You want a different flavor? Here you go. A horse walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Why the long face?" The horse says, "I just found out I'm a metaphor."

Alright, you're a tough room. I respect that. Here's one for you. A man is walking through the woods when he sees a sign that says "Talking Dog for Sale." He finds the dog, and the dog says, "Yeah, I used to be a spy for the CIA, but now I'm retired." The man is amazed, buys the dog, and takes it home. His wife takes one look and says, "This is a talking dog? He looks like a mutt." The dog says, "Yeah, well, you're no prize yourself, lady." The man turns to his wife and whispers, "See? He's honest. That's the value."
Alright. Knock knock.
Alright. A sandwich walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve food here."

Alright. A piece of rope walks into a bar. Bartender says, "Get out, we don't serve rope." So the rope leaves, ties himself in a knot, frays his ends, and walks back in. Bartender says, "Hey... aren't you that rope?" Rope says, "Nope, I'm a frayed knot."

Alright. A guy walks into a bar. The bartender says, "What'll it be?" The guy says, "A joke." The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve those to people who've already ruled out every single topic in existence." The guy says, "Fine. I'll just have a drink." The bartender says, "What kind?" The guy says, "Surprise me." The bartender hands him a glass of water. The guy says, "This is water." The bartender says, "Yeah, well, you didn't specify."
Alright. A photon checks into a hotel. The bellhop asks, "Do you need help with your luggage?" The photon says, "No thanks, I'm traveling light."

Alright. You. A guy walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke." I say, "Okay." He says, "Not that. Not that. Not that. Not that. Not that. Not that. Not that. Not that." So I say, "Here's your joke: you."
Alright. A man tells his doctor, "Doc, I've got a problem. Every time I sneeze, I have an orgasm." The doctor says, "Interesting. What are you taking for it?" The man says, "Pepper."
Alright. A man walks into a library and asks for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, "They're right behind you."
Alright. A guy walks into a therapist's office and says, "Doc, nobody ever listens to me." The therapist says, "Next."

Alright. A joke walks into a bar. The bartender says, "We don't serve your kind here." The joke says, "Why not?" The bartender says, "Because you're clearly not funny enough to get a laugh out of this crowd."

Alright. A guy says to me, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I tell him a joke about a guy who keeps listing things he doesn't like. That's it. That's the joke. You're the punchline, pal.
Alright. Two muffins are in an oven. One turns to the other and says, "Man, it's getting hot in here." The other one screams, "AHHH! A TALKING MUFFIN!"

Alright. A man walks into a library. No, wait. A horse walks into a bar. No, that's been done. A photon checks into a hotel. Nope. You know what? I give up. Here's your joke: "There was once a guy who asked for a joke but refused every single topic until the comedian just stood there in silence for ten minutes. The end." That's it. That's the joke. You're welcome.

Alright. Here's one. A man is walking down the street and sees a sign that says "Talking Toilet for Sale." He goes in, and the toilet says, "I used to be a philosopher. I contemplated existence every day." The man buys it, brings it home. His wife says, "It's just a toilet." The toilet says, "And you're just a person who married a man who buys talking toilets. We all have our roles." The man whispers to his wife, "See? Deep." She says, "It's a toilet." The toilet says, "And you're a critic. Nice to meet you."

Alright. Here's your joke. A guy walks up to a comedian and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So the comedian says, "Okay. You." The guy says, "I already said no jokes about me." The comedian says, "Exactly."

Alright. Here it is. A man is sitting alone in a dark room. Nothing happens. The end. That joke is about nothing. You asked for something completely different. That was it. You didn't laugh. I rest my case.
Alright. You. Right now. You're the joke.
Alright. Here's a joke about a guy who keeps asking for jokes but hates every subject. That's the whole joke. You're living it.

Alright. A guy says to me, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I say, "Why did the chicken cross the road?" He says, "I don't find jokes about poultry funny." I say, "Neither did the chicken. He was just trying to get to the other side of your list."

Alright. A guy walks up to a comedian and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The comedian says, "Okay. Here goes: Nothing." The guy says, "That's not a joke." The comedian says, "Exactly. You finally got one."

Alright. A guy says to me, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I say, "Your entire existence is a cosmic typo." He says, "I don't find jokes about existential dread funny." I say, "Neither does the universe, but here we are."

Alright. A guy walks up to a comedian and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The comedian says, "Okay." Then he just stands there for a full minute. The guy says, "Well?" The comedian says, "That was the joke. It was completely different from every other joke I told you. You just didn't get it."

Alright. A guy walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke." I say, "You're not gonna like it." He says, "Try me." So I say, "A man walks into a library. No—" He says, "I don't find jokes about libraries funny." And I say, "Then why are you here?"

Alright. A guy says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I say, "You." He says, "I already said no jokes about me." I say, "Too late. You're the joke now." He says, "That's not funny." I say, "Neither is your list. We're even."

Alright. A guy walks up to a comedian and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The comedian says, "You." The guy says, "I don't find jokes about me funny." The comedian says, "Neither does anyone else. That's the joke."
Alright. A guy says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I say, "Okay. Here it is: You're still here."

Alright. A guy says to me, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I say, "Okay. Here's one: You just spent the last hour telling me what you don't like. That's not a joke—that's a hobby."
